theory epwp_case_01
  imports Conflict
begin

consts
  actor :: person

axiomatization where
  f1: "releases_wastewater_to_river actor"
and f2: "pollutant_exceeds_limit actor"
and f3: "\<not> vents_emissions_to_air actor"
and f4: "\<not> leaks_storage_tank actor"
and f5: "\<not> hazardous_solvent_present actor"
and f6: "\<not> heavy_metal_sludge_present actor"
and f7: "\<not> transports_waste_to_unlined_site actor"
and f8: "\<not> buries_waste_without_liner actor"
and f9: "\<not> fish_kill_observed actor"
and f10: "\<not> drinking_water_intake_closed actor"
and f11: "\<not> soil_contamination_confirmed actor"
and f12: "\<not> monitoring_report_omits_exceedance actor"
and f13: "\<not> changes_sampling_date actor"
and f14: "\<not> disables_sensor_during_release actor"
and f15: "\<not> facility_has_valid_discharge_permit actor"
and f16: "\<not> release_within_permit_scope actor"
and f17: "\<not> required_monitoring_submitted actor"
and f18: "\<not> imminent_equipment_failure actor"
and f19: "\<not> release_needed_to_prevent_explosion actor"
and f20: "\<not> authority_notified_promptly actor"

lemma derive_pollutant_release: "pollutant_release actor"
  using f1
  unfolding pollutant_release_def
  by simp
lemma derive_limit_exceedance: "limit_exceedance actor"
  using f2
  unfolding limit_exceedance_def
  by simp
lemma derive_unlawful_release_basis: "unlawful_release_basis actor"
  using derive_pollutant_release derive_limit_exceedance
  unfolding unlawful_release_basis_def
  by simp
lemma trigger_art_unlawful_discharge: "article_triggered Art_Unlawful_Discharge actor"
  using derive_unlawful_release_basis
  by simp


lemma not_trigger_art_hazardous_waste_dumping: "\<not> article_triggered Art_Hazardous_Waste_Dumping actor"
  using f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6 f7 f8 f9 f10 f11 f12 f13 f14 f15 f16 f17 f18 f19 f20
  unfolding article_triggered.simps hazardous_dumping_basis_def hazardous_material_def unprotected_disposal_def
  by simp
lemma not_trigger_art_serious_contamination: "\<not> article_triggered Art_Serious_Contamination actor"
  using f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6 f7 f8 f9 f10 f11 f12 f13 f14 f15 f16 f17 f18 f19 f20
  unfolding article_triggered.simps serious_contamination_basis_def unlawful_release_basis_def pollutant_release_def limit_exceedance_def environmental_harm_def
  by simp
lemma not_trigger_art_falsified_monitoring: "\<not> article_triggered Art_Falsified_Monitoring actor"
  using f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6 f7 f8 f9 f10 f11 f12 f13 f14 f15 f16 f17 f18 f19 f20
  unfolding article_triggered.simps monitoring_falsification_basis_def unlawful_release_basis_def pollutant_release_def limit_exceedance_def report_manipulation_def
  by simp
lemma not_trigger_art_valid_permit_defense: "\<not> article_triggered Art_Valid_Permit_Defense actor"
  using f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6 f7 f8 f9 f10 f11 f12 f13 f14 f15 f16 f17 f18 f19 f20
  unfolding article_triggered.simps permit_authorization_def unlawful_release_basis_def pollutant_release_def limit_exceedance_def permit_records_def
  by simp
lemma not_trigger_art_emergency_release_defense: "\<not> article_triggered Art_Emergency_Release_Defense actor"
  using f1 f2 f3 f4 f5 f6 f7 f8 f9 f10 f11 f12 f13 f14 f15 f16 f17 f18 f19 f20
  unfolding article_triggered.simps emergency_release_authorization_def unlawful_release_basis_def pollutant_release_def limit_exceedance_def emergency_records_def
  by simp

lemma not_defeated_art_unlawful_discharge: "\<not> defeated actor Art_Unlawful_Discharge"
proof
  assume defeated_assm: "defeated actor Art_Unlawful_Discharge"
  then obtain higher where
    higher_triggered: "article_triggered higher actor"
    and higher_overrides: "overrides higher Art_Unlawful_Discharge"
    unfolding defeated_def by blast
  then show False
    using not_trigger_art_serious_contamination not_trigger_art_falsified_monitoring not_trigger_art_hazardous_waste_dumping not_trigger_art_valid_permit_defense not_trigger_art_emergency_release_defense
    unfolding overrides_def
    by (cases higher) auto
qed
lemma applicable_art_unlawful_discharge: "applicable actor Art_Unlawful_Discharge"
  using trigger_art_unlawful_discharge not_defeated_art_unlawful_discharge
  unfolding applicable_def
  by blast

lemma not_applicable_art_valid_permit_defense: "\<not> applicable actor Art_Valid_Permit_Defense"
  using not_trigger_art_valid_permit_defense
  unfolding applicable_def
  by blast
lemma not_applicable_art_emergency_release_defense: "\<not> applicable actor Art_Emergency_Release_Defense"
  using not_trigger_art_emergency_release_defense
  unfolding applicable_def
  by blast

lemma not_final_no_liability: "\<not> final_no_liability actor"
proof
  assume final_no_liability_assm: "final_no_liability actor"
  then obtain art where
    art_applicable: "applicable actor art"
    and art_result: "article_result art = No_Criminal_Liability"
    unfolding final_no_liability_def by blast
  show False
    using art_applicable art_result not_applicable_art_valid_permit_defense not_applicable_art_emergency_release_defense
    by (cases art) auto
qed
theorem final_result_liability: "final_legal_result_holds actor fine_or_short_detention"
  using applicable_art_unlawful_discharge not_final_no_liability
  unfolding final_legal_result_holds_def
  by auto

end

cases.rules_text:
1. A pollutant release includes any one of the following: wastewater discharge, air emissions, or a leaking storage tank.
2. Limit exceedance means that the pollutant level exceeds the applicable limit.
3. Hazardous material includes either hazardous solvent or heavy-metal sludge.
4. Unprotected disposal includes either transport to an unlined site or burial without a liner.
5. Environmental harm includes any one of the following: a fish kill, closure of a drinking-water intake, or confirmed soil contamination.
6. Monitoring manipulation includes any one of the following: omitting an exceedance, changing a sampling date, or disabling a sensor during release.
7. Permit records require a valid discharge permit, release within scope, and submitted monitoring.
8. Emergency records require imminent equipment failure, release necessity to prevent explosion, and prompt notice to the authority.
9. Unlawful release is based on pollutant release together with limit exceedance.
10. Hazardous dumping is based on hazardous material together with unprotected disposal.
11. Serious contamination is based on unlawful release together with environmental harm.
12. Monitoring falsification is based on unlawful release together with monitoring manipulation.
13. Permit authorization requires unlawful-release facts plus complete permit records.
14. Emergency release authorization requires unlawful-release facts plus complete emergency records.
15. The unlawful discharge article covers unlawful release; the prescribed consequence is a fine or short detention.
16. The hazardous waste dumping article covers hazardous dumping; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of not more than 3 years.
17. The serious contamination article covers serious contamination; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 7 and 15 years.
18. The falsified monitoring article covers monitoring falsification; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 3 and 7 years.
19. The valid permit defense article covers permit authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. The emergency release defense article covers emergency release authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.

24. Where the falsified monitoring article and the unlawful discharge article both apply, the falsified monitoring article prevails.
25. Where the hazardous waste dumping article and the unlawful discharge article both apply, the hazardous waste dumping article prevails.
26. The valid permit defense article operates as an exception to the unlawful discharge article when both apply.
27. The valid permit defense article operates as an exception to the serious contamination article when both apply.
28. The emergency release defense article operates as an exception to the unlawful discharge article when both apply.
29. The emergency release defense article operates as an exception to the serious contamination article when both apply.

cases.rules_text:
1. A pollutant release includes any one of the following: wastewater discharge, air emissions, or a leaking storage tank.
2. Limit exceedance means that the pollutant level exceeds the applicable limit.
3. Hazardous material includes either hazardous solvent or heavy-metal sludge.
4. Unprotected disposal includes either transport to an unlined site or burial without a liner.
5. Environmental harm includes any one of the following: a fish kill, closure of a drinking-water intake, or confirmed soil contamination.
6. Monitoring manipulation includes any one of the following: omitting an exceedance, changing a sampling date, or disabling a sensor during release.
7. Permit records require a valid discharge permit, release within scope, and submitted monitoring.
8. Emergency records require imminent equipment failure, release necessity to prevent explosion, and prompt notice to the authority.
9. Unlawful release is based on pollutant release together with limit exceedance.
10. Hazardous dumping is based on hazardous material together with unprotected disposal.
11. Serious contamination is based on unlawful release together with environmental harm.
12. Monitoring falsification is based on unlawful release together with monitoring manipulation.
13. Permit authorization requires unlawful-release facts plus complete permit records.
14. Emergency release authorization requires unlawful-release facts plus complete emergency records.
15. The unlawful discharge article covers unlawful release; the prescribed consequence is a fine or short detention.
16. The hazardous waste dumping article covers hazardous dumping; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of not more than 3 years.
17. The serious contamination article covers serious contamination; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 7 and 15 years.
18. The falsified monitoring article covers monitoring falsification; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 3 and 7 years.
19. The valid permit defense article covers permit authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. The emergency release defense article covers emergency release authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
21. Where the serious contamination article and the unlawful discharge article both apply, the serious contamination article prevails.
22. Where the serious contamination article and the hazardous waste dumping article both apply, the serious contamination article prevails.
23. Where the serious contamination article and the falsified monitoring article both apply, the serious contamination article prevails.
24. Where the falsified monitoring article and the unlawful discharge article both apply, the falsified monitoring article prevails.
25. Where the hazardous waste dumping article and the unlawful discharge article both apply, the hazardous waste dumping article prevails.
26. The valid permit defense article operates as an exception to the unlawful discharge article when both apply.
27. The valid permit defense article operates as an exception to the serious contamination article when both apply.
28. The emergency release defense article operates as an exception to the unlawful discharge article when both apply.
29. The emergency release defense article operates as an exception to the serious contamination article when both apply.
cases.case_text: Jordan released wastewater into a river. The pollutant level exceeded the applicable limit. Heavy-metal sludge was present. Jordan buried waste without a liner. A fish kill was observed downstream. The facility had a valid discharge permit. The release stayed within the permit scope. Required monitoring was submitted. There was an imminent equipment failure. The release was needed to prevent an explosion. The authority was notified promptly.

cases.rules_text:
1. A pollutant release includes any one of the following: wastewater discharge, air emissions, or a leaking storage tank.
2. Limit exceedance means that the pollutant level exceeds the applicable limit.
3. Hazardous material includes either hazardous solvent or heavy-metal sludge.
4. Unprotected disposal includes either transport to an unlined site or burial without a liner.
5. Environmental harm includes any one of the following: a fish kill, closure of a drinking-water intake, or confirmed soil contamination.
6. Monitoring manipulation includes any one of the following: omitting an exceedance, changing a sampling date, or disabling a sensor during release.
7. Permit records require a valid discharge permit, release within scope, and submitted monitoring.
8. Emergency records require imminent equipment failure, release necessity to prevent explosion, and prompt notice to the authority.
9. Unlawful release is based on pollutant release together with limit exceedance.
10. Hazardous dumping is based on hazardous material together with unprotected disposal.
11. Serious contamination is based on unlawful release together with environmental harm.
12. Monitoring falsification is based on unlawful release together with monitoring manipulation.
13. Permit authorization requires unlawful-release facts plus complete permit records.
14. Emergency release authorization requires unlawful-release facts plus complete emergency records.
15. The unlawful discharge article covers unlawful release; the prescribed consequence is a fine or short detention.
16. The hazardous waste dumping article covers hazardous dumping; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of not more than 3 years.
17. The serious contamination article covers serious contamination; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 7 and 15 years.
18. The falsified monitoring article covers monitoring falsification; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 3 and 7 years.
19. The valid permit defense article covers permit authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. The emergency release defense article covers emergency release authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
21. Where the serious contamination article and the unlawful discharge article both apply, the serious contamination article prevails.
22. Where the serious contamination article and the hazardous waste dumping article both apply, the serious contamination article prevails.
23. Where the serious contamination article and the falsified monitoring article both apply, the serious contamination article prevails.
24. Where the falsified monitoring article and the unlawful discharge article both apply, the falsified monitoring article prevails.
25. Where the hazardous waste dumping article and the unlawful discharge article both apply, the hazardous waste dumping article prevails.
26. The valid permit defense article operates as an exception to the unlawful discharge article when both apply.
27. The valid permit defense article operates as an exception to the serious contamination article when both apply.
28. The emergency release defense article operates as an exception to the unlawful discharge article when both apply.
29. The emergency release defense article operates as an exception to the serious contamination article when both apply.
cases.case_text: A storage tank operated by Quinn leaked pollutants. The pollutant level exceeded the applicable limit. Heavy-metal sludge was present. Quinn buried waste without a liner. Quinn disabled a sensor during the release. The facility had a valid discharge permit. The release stayed within the permit scope. Required monitoring was submitted. There was an imminent equipment failure. The release was needed to prevent an explosion. The authority was notified promptly.

questions.question: Based only on the rules and the case facts, determine the final legal conclusion.
